content.body: Green chemistry designs chemical processes to minimise environmental impact. The 12 principles guide chemists to prevent pollution at source rather than clean it up afterwards.

12 Principles of Green Chemistry (key ones):
1. Prevention: Better to prevent waste than treat it
2. Atom economy: Maximise incorporation of materials into product
3. Catalysis: Use catalysts, not stoichiometric reagents
4. Renewable feedstocks: Use renewable raw materials
5. Design for degradation: Products should break down after use

Atom Economy = (Molecular mass of desired product / Total molecular mass of reactants) × 100%

content.title: Atom Economy Calculation
content.body:
Reaction: CH₄ + 2Cl₂ → CH₂Cl₂ + 2HCl
Desired product: CH₂Cl₂ (MW = 85)
Total reactants: CH₄(16) + 2Cl₂(142) = 158
Atom Economy = 85/158 × 100 = 53.8%
The other 46.2% becomes waste HCl

questions.question: Atom economy measures:
options: How much of reactant mass ends up in the desired product
questions.answer: How much of reactant mass ends up in the desired product
questions.explanation: Atom economy = (mass of desired product/total mass of reactants) × 100%

questions.question: Which has higher atom economy?
questions.explanation: Addition reactions have 100% atom economy — all atoms from reactants end up in the product

questions.question: The first principle of green chemistry is:
questions.explanation: The first principle: it is better to prevent waste than treat it

questions.question: Using a catalyst improves green chemistry because:
options: It is consumed in the reaction
It reduces energy use and is reusable
questions.answer: It reduces energy use and is reusable
questions.explanation: Catalysts lower activation energy (less energy needed) and are not consumed

questions.question: A biodegradable solvent is preferred because:
options: It does not persist in environment
questions.answer: It does not persist in environment
questions.explanation: Biodegradable solvents break down naturally, reducing long-term environmental impact
